title: Backend micro-step: make legacy API inventory complete and UserProfile optional-field documentation consistent

requirements.text: In `backend/main.mo`, update/refine the existing `PUBLIC ACTOR API & TYPES (BACKWARD COMPATIBILITY: LEGACY INVENTORY)` comment block so it inventories **all currently exposed public canister methods** and key public types present in the file, and explicitly marks them as “must remain backward compatible” for this micro-step (documentation-only; do not rename/remove methods or types).

quotes: In `backend/main.mo`, update/refine the existing “PUBLIC ACTOR API & TYPES (BACKWARD COMPATIBILITY: LEGACY INVENTORY)” comment block so it inventories **all currently exposed public canister methods** and key public types present in the file, and explicitly marks them as “must remain backward compatible” for this micro-step (documentation-only; do not rename/remove methods or types).

acceptanceCriteria: `backend/main.mo` contains an updated inventory comment that includes every `public`/`public query`/`public shared` function defined in the actor (e.g., including items currently omitted such as `getAllLicenses`, `getMyLicenseStatus`, and `getLicenseStatus`, if present).
The inventory comment also lists the key public types that are part of the exposed surface in this file (as they exist today), without changing any type definitions.
No public method names, signatures, or return types are changed as part of this requirement.

requirements.text: In `backend/main.mo`, make the backward-compatibility documentation internally consistent by ensuring the comment immediately above the `UserProfile` type explicitly states that the Phase 1 fields `planTier`, `botPublicKey`, and `bot_id` are intentionally OPTIONAL for backward compatibility with previously stored profiles and older frontend calls, and remove/adjust any wording that could be interpreted as those fields being required.

quotes: In `backend/main.mo`, make the backward-compatibility documentation internally consistent by ensuring the comment immediately above the `UserProfile` type explicitly states that the Phase 1 fields `planTier`, `botPublicKey`, and `bot_id` are intentionally OPTIONAL for backward compatibility with previously stored profiles and older frontend calls, and remove/adjust any wording that could be interpreted as those fields being required.

acceptanceCriteria: The comment block immediately above `type UserProfile = { ... }` clearly states that `planTier`, `botPublicKey`, and `bot_id` are optional for backward compatibility.
There is no contradictory wording elsewhere in the nearby legacy inventory/comments implying those fields are required.
No changes are made to any public method signatures.

requirements.text: In `backend/main.mo`, verify (and if necessary, minimally adjust) any `UserProfile` record literals/constructors so that when a profile is constructed without an explicit bot id, it sets `bot_id = null` explicitly (do not rely on missing-field inference), preserving compatibility for callers that omit `bot_id`.

quotes: In `backend/main.mo`, verify (and if necessary, minimally adjust) any `UserProfile` record literals/constructors so that when a profile is constructed without an explicit bot id, it sets `bot_id = null` explicitly (do not rely on missing-field inference), preserving compatibility for callers that omit `bot_id`.

acceptanceCriteria: All `UserProfile` record literals in `backend/main.mo` explicitly include a `bot_id` field assignment (`null` when not intentionally provided).
The canister compiles with no missing-field errors related to `bot_id`.
No new persistence/migration logic is introduced (no `stable` refactors, no `system preupgrade`/`system postupgrade` additions).

constraints: Backend must remain a single-actor architecture with all Motoko logic in `backend/main.mo`.
Do not change any existing public method signatures or rename/remove public methods/types in this micro-step.
Do not introduce new persistence/migration logic in this micro-step (no `stable` refactors and no `system preupgrade`/`system postupgrade`).
nonGoals: Implementing the full Phase 1 stable-memory users registry, bot_id index, audit logs ledger, or Ed25519 verification (beyond documentation/compatibility edits).
Changing any frontend behavior or UI.
